254	// DefaultProfileLadders returns the built-in profile escalation policy.
255	func DefaultProfileLadders() map[string][]string {
256		out := make(map[string][]string, len(defaultProfileLadders))
257		for profile, ladder := range defaultProfileLadders {
258			out[profile] = append([]string(nil), ladder...)
259		}

263	// ResolvedLadder returns the escalation ladder for the named profile. If
264	// ProfileLadders contains an entry for profile, that wins. Otherwise falls
265	// back to the deprecated ProfilePriority for the default profile only. If
266	// neither is set, returns the shipped FEAT-006 profile ladder.
267	func (r *RoutingConfig) ResolvedLadder(profile string) []string {
268		if profile == "" {
269			profile = "default"
270		}
271		if r == nil {
272			if ladder, ok := defaultProfileLadders[profile]; ok {
273				return append([]string(nil), ladder...)
274			}
275			return append([]string(nil), defaultProfileLadders["default"]...)
276		}
277		if r.ProfileLadders != nil {
278			if ladder, ok := r.ProfileLadders[profile]; ok && len(ladder) > 0 {
279				return append([]string(nil), ladder...)
280			}
281		}
282		if profile == "default" && len(r.ProfilePriority) > 0 {
283			return append([]string(nil), r.ProfilePriority...)
284		}
285		if ladder, ok := defaultProfileLadders[profile]; ok {
286			return append([]string(nil), ladder...)
287		}
288		return append([]string(nil), defaultProfileLadders["default"]...)
289	}

42		assert.Equal(t, []string{"cheap"}, cfg.Routing.ProfileLadders["cheap"])
43		require.Contains(t, cfg.Routing.ProfileLadders, "fast")
44		assert.Equal(t, []string{"fast", "smart"}, cfg.Routing.ProfileLadders["fast"])
45		require.Contains(t, cfg.Routing.ProfileLadders, "smart")
46		assert.Equal(t, []string{"smart"}, cfg.Routing.ProfileLadders["smart"])

48		assert.Equal(t, "qwen/qwen3.6", cfg.Routing.ModelOverrides["cheap"])

124	// TestRoutingConfigResolvedLadder captures the precedence rule:
125	// ProfileLadders wins when a profile-specific entry exists; legacy
126	// ProfilePriority is the default-profile fallback; the shipped FEAT-006
127	// defaults are used when neither config form is set.
128	func TestRoutingConfigResolvedLadder(t *testing.T) {
129		cases := []struct {
130			name    string
131			cfg     *RoutingConfig
132			profile string
133			want    []string
134		}{
135			{
136				name: "profile_ladders wins for matched profile",
137				cfg: &RoutingConfig{
138					ProfileLadders: map[string][]string{
139						"default": {"cheap", "smart"},
140					},
141					ProfilePriority: []string{"fast"}, // should be ignored
142				},
143				profile: "default",
144				want:    []string{"cheap", "smart"},
145			},
146			{
147				name: "falls through to legacy profile_priority when no ladder entry",
148				cfg: &RoutingConfig{
149					ProfileLadders: map[string][]string{
150						"cheap": {"cheap"},
151					},
152					ProfilePriority: []string{"cheap", "standard"},
153				},
154				profile: "default",
155				want:    []string{"cheap", "standard"},
156			},
157			{
158				name: "legacy only aliases default",
159				cfg: &RoutingConfig{
160					ProfilePriority: []string{"cheap"},
161				},
162				profile: "default",
163				want:    []string{"cheap"},
164			},
165			{
166				name: "legacy only does not override named shipped profile",
167				cfg: &RoutingConfig{
168					ProfilePriority: []string{"cheap"},
169				},
170				profile: "smart",
171				want:    []string{"smart"},
172			},
173			{
174				name:    "nil routing",
175				cfg:     nil,
176				profile: "default",
177				want:    []string{"cheap", "standard", "smart"},
178			},
179			{
180				name: "empty everywhere uses shipped default",
181				cfg: &RoutingConfig{
182					ProfileLadders:  map[string][]string{},
183					ProfilePriority: nil,
184				},
185				profile: "default",
186				want:    []string{"cheap", "standard", "smart"},
187			},
188			{
189				name:    "shipped fast profile",
190				cfg:     nil,
191				profile: "fast",
192				want:    []string{"fast", "smart"},
193			},
194		}
195		for _, tc := range cases {
196			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
197				assert.Equal(t, tc.want, tc.cfg.ResolvedLadder(tc.profile))
198			})
199		}
200	}

1628	func (f *CommandFactory) runAgentExecuteLoop(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
1629		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
1630		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
1631		fromRev, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("from")
1632		harness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("harness")
1633		model, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model")
1634		profile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("profile")
1635		provider, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("provider")
1636		modelRef, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model-ref")
1637		effort, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("effort")
1638		once, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("once")
1639		pollInterval, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("poll-interval")
1640		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
1641		local, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("local")
1642		noReview, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-review")
1643		reviewHarness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-harness")
1644		reviewModel, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-model")
1645		minTier, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("min-tier")
1646		maxTier, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("max-tier")
1647		noAdaptiveMinTier, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-adaptive-min-tier")
1648		adaptiveWindow, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("adaptive-min-tier-window")
1649		maxCostUSD, _ := cmd.Flags().GetFloat64("max-cost")
1650		escalate, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("escalate")
1651	
1652		// Adaptive min-tier: when the operator did not pin --min-tier and adaptation
1653		// is not disabled, consult trailing cheap-tier success rate and promote to
1654		// standard when the cheap tier has been nearly pure waste. This saves the
1655		// queue from burning time and budget on attempts that almost never succeed.
1656		if minTier == "" && !noAdaptiveMinTier {
1657			adaptive := escalation.AdaptiveMinTier(projectRoot, adaptiveWindow, agent.ResolveModelTier)
1658			if adaptive.Skipped {
1659				minTier = string(adaptive.Tier)
1660				fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(),
1661					"adaptive min-tier: skipping cheap tier (trailing success rate %.2f over %d attempts; threshold %.2f) — min-tier=%s\n",
1662					adaptive.CheapSuccessRate, adaptive.CheapAttempts, escalation.AdaptiveMinTierThreshold, minTier)
1663			}
1664		}
1665	
1666		// If --local, run inline; otherwise submit to running ddx server
1667		if !local {
1668			return f.executeLoopWithServer(cmd, projectRoot, harness, model, profile, provider, modelRef, effort, once, pollInterval, asJSON, noReview, reviewHarness, reviewModel, minTier, maxTier)
1669		}
1670	
1671		// Pre-flight: validate harness availability and model compatibility
1672		// before claiming any beads. This surfaces errors like "claude binary
1673		// not found" or "vidar is an agent preset, not a claude model" before
1674		// any bead status changes hands.
1675		if err := agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService(cmd.Context(), f.WorkingDir, harness, model, provider, modelRef); err != nil {
1676			return fmt.Errorf("execute-loop: %w", err)
1677		}
1678	
1679		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"))
1680	
1681		// Structured progress sink for this loop run. Events emitted at
1682		// loop.start, bead.claimed, bead.result, and loop.end land here so
1683		// log aggregators (FormatSessionLogLines, `ddx server workers log`)
1684		// can parse the same JSONL envelope used by harness session logs.
1685		loopSessionID := fmt.Sprintf("agent-loop-%d", time.Now().UnixNano())
1686		loopLogDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, agent.DefaultLogDir)
1687		_ = os.MkdirAll(loopLogDir, 0o755)
1688		loopLogPath := filepath.Join(loopLogDir, loopSessionID+".jsonl")
1689		var loopSink io.Writer
1690		if f, err := os.OpenFile(loopLogPath, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_APPEND, 0o644); err == nil {
1691			loopSink = f
1692			defer f.Close() //nolint:errcheck
1693		}
1694	
1695		// Start session log tailer so the user sees progress during agent execution
1696		tailCtx, tailCancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
1697		go agent.TailSessionLogs(tailCtx, projectRoot, cmd.OutOrStdout())
1698	
1699		// Instantiate a process-local LandCoordinator so --local uses the same
1700		// single-writer land path as the server worker. Stopped on function exit.
1701		localCoord := serverpkg.NewLocalLandCoordinator(projectRoot, agent.RealLandingGitOps{})
1702		defer localCoord.Stop()
1703	
1704		// Build post-merge reviewer unless --no-review is set.
1705		// Reviewer is on-by-default: runs at smart tier after every successful merge.
1706		var reviewer agent.BeadReviewer
1707		if !noReview {
1708			reviewer = &agent.DefaultBeadReviewer{
1709				ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
1710				BeadStore:   bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
1711				Harness:     reviewHarness,
1712				Model:       reviewModel,
1713			}
1714		}
1715	
1716		// escalationEnabled is true when neither --harness nor --model is pinned.
1717		// In that case the executor iterates through tiers (cheap → standard →
1718		// smart) and escalates on failure. When either flag is set, the executor
1719		// runs a single attempt with the specified harness/model.
1720		profile = agent.NormalizeRoutingProfile(profile)
1721		cfg, _ := config.LoadWithWorkingDir(projectRoot)
1722		var routingCfg *config.RoutingConfig
1723		if cfg != nil && cfg.Agent != nil {
1724			routingCfg = cfg.Agent.Routing
1725		}
1726	
1727		// ddx-755f5881: tier-ladder escalation is opt-in via --escalate.
1728		// The default path goes through singleTierAttempt with one ResolveRoute call
1729		// and lets upstream pick the harness/model — no DDx-side ladder iteration,
1730		// no ResolveTierModelRef lookup, no preference-order fallback.
1731		escalationEnabled := escalate && harness == "" && model == ""
1732	
1733		// Cost-cap state shared by both the single-attempt and tier-escalation
1734		// paths. Accumulated billed spend (excluding local and subscription
1735		// providers) above --max-cost trips the cap and halts further bead
1736		// claiming. See escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD / CountsTowardCostCap.
1737		costCap := escalation.NewCostCapTracker(maxCostUSD, func(harnessName string) bool {
1738			// Resolve the harness's billing class via the service. Treat any
1739			// resolution error as "count by default" (safe — we'd rather cap
1740			// early than silently overshoot).
1741			svc, svcErr := agent.NewServiceFromWorkDir(f.WorkingDir)
1742			if svcErr != nil {
1743				return true
1744			}
1745			infos, err := svc.ListHarnesses(context.Background())
1746			if err != nil {
1747				return true

1	# Changelog
2	
3	All notable changes to DDx are documented in this file.
4	
5	## [Unreleased]
6	
7	### Fix: `latency_ms` in claude harness traces now reflects per-call duration
8	
9	Previously every `llm.response` event in `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-claude-*.jsonl`
10	had `latency_ms == elapsed_ms` because the claude stream parser wrote the
11	cumulative wall-clock value into both fields. Per-call LLM latency was
12	therefore unrecoverable from captured traces.
13	
14	The parser now tracks the time the most recent LLM request was sent (session
15	start, then reset on each `user`/tool_result event) and emits
16	`latency_ms = now - lastRequestSentAt` for each assistant turn. `elapsed_ms`
17	remains cumulative, so for any event past turn 1 the two values diverge.
18	
19	Legacy traces produced before this commit have `latency_ms == elapsed_ms` and
20	should be ignored by latency-analysis tooling for runs predating the fix.
21	
22	Regression coverage: `TestParseClaudeStreamLatencyIsPerCall` in
23	`cli/internal/agent/claude_stream_test.go` drives two assistant turns through
24	the parser with real sleeps and asserts (a) `latency_ms != elapsed_ms` for
25	turn 2, (b) the second-turn latency excludes the prior turn and tool window,
26	and (c) the sum of per-call latencies does not exceed the final event's
27	`elapsed_ms`.
28	
29	### execute-loop: structured `declined_needs_decomposition` outcome
30	
31	Adds a first-class outcome distinct from `no_changes` and `execution_failed`
32	for executors that conclude a bead is too large to deliver in one pass and
33	must first be split into sub-beads. Previously this case was conveyed via
34	free-form text in `no_changes_rationale`, and the loop kept re-attempting the
35	bead under a short cooldown — burning tokens to re-derive the same
36	recommendation across runs.
37	
38	The new contract:
39	
40	- `ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition` (`declined_needs_decomposition`)
41	  is a new value of the execute-bead status. Executors set it together with
42	  `DecompositionRecommendation []string` (recommended sub-bead titles) and
43	  optional `DecompositionRationale string` on `ExecuteBeadReport`.
44	- The execute-loop responds by appending a structured
45	  `decomposition-recommendation` event (JSON body carrying the rationale and
46	  recommended sub-beads) and parking the bead with a 365-day cooldown so
47	  subsequent loop iterations do not re-attempt it. The bead remains open;
48	  its status field is unchanged.
49	- A first-class CLI for the cooldown surface lands as `ddx bead cooldown
50	  show <id>` and `ddx bead cooldown clear <id>`. Operators should reach for

package config

import (
	"io"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

// TestLoadConfigHardErrorsOnDefaultHarness covers AC #1 of bead
// ddx-87fb72c2: a config carrying agent.routing.default_harness must
// fail to load with a migration message. The CLI surfaces the error to
// the user; the loader's exit code is non-zero (the *cobra.Command path
// returns the error to its caller).
func TestLoadConfigHardErrorsOnDefaultHarness(t *testing.T) {
	tempDir := t.TempDir()
	ddxDir := filepath.Join(tempDir, ".ddx")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(`version: "1.0"
library:
  path: "./library"
  repository:
    url: "https://github.com/test/repo"
    branch: "main"
agent:
  routing:
    default_harness: claude
`), 0644))

	_, err := LoadWithWorkingDir(tempDir)
	require.Error(t, err, "loading must fail when agent.routing.default_harness is present")

	migErr, ok := err.(*RoutingMigrationError)
	require.True(t, ok, "expected RoutingMigrationError, got %T: %v", err, err)
	assert.Equal(t, "agent.routing.default_harness", migErr.Field)

	msg := err.Error()
	assert.Contains(t, msg, "agent.routing.default_harness")
	assert.Contains(t, msg, "removed")
	assert.Contains(t, msg, "docs/migrations/routing-config.md")
}

// TestLoadConfigWarnsOnceForOptInRoutingFields covers AC #2: configs
// containing profile_ladders + model_overrides load successfully but
// emit a one-time process warning, AND the default execute path does
// not consult those fields. Consultation only happens when --escalate /
// --override-model are explicitly passed.
func TestLoadConfigWarnsOnceForOptInRoutingFields(t *testing.T) {
	ResetRoutingDeprecationWarnings()
	t.Cleanup(ResetRoutingDeprecationWarnings)

	tempDir := t.TempDir()
	ddxDir := filepath.Join(tempDir, ".ddx")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(`version: "1.0"
library:
  path: "./library"
  repository:
    url: "https://github.com/test/repo"
    branch: "main"
agent:
  routing:
    profile_ladders:
      default: [cheap, standard, smart]
    model_overrides:
      cheap: qwen/qwen3.6
`), 0644))

	oldStderr := os.Stderr
	r, w, err := os.Pipe()
	require.NoError(t, err)
	os.Stderr = w
	t.Cleanup(func() { os.Stderr = oldStderr })

	cfg, loadErr := LoadWithWorkingDir(tempDir)
	require.NoError(t, loadErr)
	require.NotNil(t, cfg)

	// Calling load a second time must NOT re-emit the same warning.
	_, _ = LoadWithWorkingDir(tempDir)

	require.NoError(t, w.Close())
	os.Stderr = oldStderr
	out, err := io.ReadAll(r)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	stderrStr := string(out)

	assert.Contains(t, stderrStr, "agent.routing.profile_ladders is opt-in")
	assert.Contains(t, stderrStr, "agent.routing.model_overrides is opt-in")
	assert.Contains(t, stderrStr, "--escalate")
	assert.Contains(t, stderrStr, "--override-model")
	// Second load does not duplicate the warning lines.
	assert.Equal(t, 1, strings.Count(stderrStr, "agent.routing.profile_ladders is opt-in"),
		"profile_ladders warning must fire once per process")
	assert.Equal(t, 1, strings.Count(stderrStr, "agent.routing.model_overrides is opt-in"),
		"model_overrides warning must fire once per process")

	// Config still parses these fields so opt-in flags can consult them.
	require.NotNil(t, cfg.Agent)
	require.NotNil(t, cfg.Agent.Routing)
	require.Contains(t, cfg.Agent.Routing.ProfileLadders, "default")
	assert.Equal(t, "qwen/qwen3.6", cfg.Agent.Routing.ModelOverrides["cheap"])
}

input.new_string:
## [Unreleased]

### Breaking changes

#### Removed: `agent.routing.default_harness`

The `agent.routing.default_harness` field has been removed from `.ddx/config.yaml`
(bead ddx-87fb72c2). DDx now refuses to load any config that still carries it
and prints a migration pointer instead.

Why: `default_harness` was a silent fallback that competed with the top-level
`agent.harness` setting and the live route-resolution call graph. It produced
confusing routing decisions when the upstream service had no viable provider for
the requested profile. The endpoint-first redesign (epic ddx-fdd3ea36) makes
harness selection an explicit decision per dispatch — a config-level "default"
no longer fits.

Migration: delete the field. The top-level `agent.harness` is still honored as
a tie-break preference on the resolution path (NOT a default override). See
[docs/migrations/routing-config.md](docs/migrations/routing-config.md).

#### Opt-in: `agent.routing.profile_ladders` and `agent.routing.model_overrides`

These fields are no longer consulted on the default execute path. They are now
explicit opt-in:

- `profile_ladders` is consulted only when `--escalate` is passed.
- `model_overrides` is consulted only when the new `--override-model` flag is
  passed.

Configs that still set these fields will load successfully but emit a one-time
process warning at config-load time. To silence the warning, either pass the
opt-in flag on the relevant invocation or remove the field. See the migration
guide for details.

### Fix: `latency_ms` in claude harness traces now reflects per-call duration

# Migration: routing-config deprecation (bead ddx-87fb72c2)

This guide covers the breaking change to `agent.routing.*` in
`.ddx/config.yaml`. The change ships as part of epic `ddx-fdd3ea36`
(endpoint-first routing).

## Summary

| Field                              | Status         | Action                       |
| ---------------------------------- | -------------- | ---------------------------- |
| `agent.routing.default_harness`    | **Removed**    | Delete the field             |
| `agent.routing.profile_ladders`    | Opt-in only    | Consulted only with `--escalate`        |
| `agent.routing.model_overrides`    | Opt-in only    | Consulted only with `--override-model`  |
| `agent.harness` (top-level)        | Kept           | Tie-break preference, not a default override |
| `agent.routing.profile_priority`   | Already deprecated | Still parsed; warns at load |

## `agent.routing.default_harness` → removed

The field has been deleted from the schema. DDx now rejects configs that
still carry it with a hard error at startup and a pointer back to this
guide. Exit code is non-zero.

### Before

```yaml
agent:
  harness: claude
  routing:
    default_harness: agent
```

### After

```yaml
agent:
  harness: claude
```

The top-level `agent.harness` field is preserved and remains honored as
a tie-break preference during route resolution. It is **not** a silent
default override.

## `agent.routing.profile_ladders` → opt-in via `--escalate`

The default execute path no longer iterates the ladder. A single
`ResolveRoute` call drives each attempt; `profile_ladders` is consulted
only when the operator explicitly passes `--escalate` to enable
tier-ladder escalation semantics.

If your config still sets `profile_ladders`, DDx emits a one-time
process warning at config-load time:

```
warning: agent.routing.profile_ladders is opt-in.
It is consulted only when --escalate is passed; the default execute
path ignores it. See docs/migrations/routing-config.md.
```

To silence the warning either drop the field, or pass `--escalate` on
the runs where you want tier escalation.

## `agent.routing.model_overrides` → opt-in via `--override-model`

The same treatment as `profile_ladders`. The default execute path
ignores `model_overrides`. Pass the new `--override-model` flag to opt
into per-tier override consultation:

```
ddx agent execute-loop --escalate --override-model
```

A one-time process warning fires at config-load time when the field is
set without invocation.

## Test commands

```bash
# Reject a config with the removed field:
echo 'version: "1.0"
library:
  path: ./library
  repository:
    url: https://example.com/repo
    branch: main
agent:
  routing:
    default_harness: claude' > /tmp/bad.yaml
DDX_PROJECT_ROOT=/tmp ddx status   # exits non-zero with migration message
```

## Background

Prior to this change, three competing surfaces dictated harness
selection: the top-level `agent.harness` field, the routing block's
`default_harness`, and the live route-resolution call graph. The
endpoint-first redesign (epic ddx-fdd3ea36) collapses harness selection
into a single explicit decision per dispatch: route resolution is the
source of truth, and `agent.harness` is the operator's tie-break. The
ladder + overrides knobs remain available for operators who explicitly
want escalation or model substitution, but they are no longer
consulted by default.

feat(config)!: deprecate routing-config; remove default_harness, opt-in ladders/overrides [ddx-87fb72c2]

Breaking change covering D4 of the endpoint-first routing redesign
(epic ddx-fdd3ea36). agent.routing.default_harness is removed from
the schema; configs that still carry it fail to load with a friendly
migration message. agent.routing.profile_ladders and
agent.routing.model_overrides are now opt-in: the default execute
path no longer consults them, and a one-time process warning fires
at config-load when they are set. Consult them by passing --escalate
(ladders) or the new --override-model flag (overrides).

The top-level agent.harness setting is preserved as a tie-break
preference for harness selection, NOT a default override.

CHANGELOG records the breaking change; docs/migrations/routing-config.md
walks operators through the rename. Tests cover the hard-error path
and the one-time-warning + opt-in mechanics.
EOF
)"

data.text:
Bead complete. Commit `fab54a93` covers all five AC items:

1. **Hard error on `default_harness`** — `cli/internal/config/config.go` `checkRoutingMigration` returns `*RoutingMigrationError` before schema validation; `TestLoadConfigHardErrorsOnDefaultHarness` asserts the error and migration message.
2. **One-time warning + opt-in mechanics** — `warnRoutingOnce` (sync.Map of sync.Once) emits the warning once per process; `--escalate`/`--override-model` flags gate consultation; `TestLoadConfigWarnsOnceForOptInRoutingFields` covers the warn-once and parse-survival path; `TestAgentExecuteLoopOverrideModelFlagRegistered` covers the new flag.
3. **Schema removal** — `DefaultHarness` field stripped from `RoutingConfig` struct, `default_harness` property dropped from `cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json`, `.ddx/config.yaml` sample purged.
4. **CHANGELOG** — new "Breaking changes" subsection in `[Unreleased]` with migration pointer.
5. **Migration guide** — `docs/migrations/routing-config.md` written.
